flags.concern: Shot-group replacement targeting: Recurring debt: the plan keys the switch action by `shotId`, but `useShotGroups()` can produce multiple distinct groups for the same shot, so the replacement target is ambiguous and can hit the wrong row or clip set.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:79-130` can emit multiple groups per shot, while the planned callbacks only pass `shotId` from `TimelineCanvas`.

flags.concern: Asset registration contract: the proposed `registerGenerationAsset()` call uses an unsupported `mimeType` field, so final videos may not be registered as video assets unless the plan passes `variantType: 'video'` or `metadata.content_type`.
flags.evidence: `GenerationDropData` in `src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts:48-55` has no `mimeType`, and `useAssetManagement.ts:71-85` infers media type only from `metadata.content_type`, `variantType`, or the URL.

flags.concern: Verification coverage: the planned checks do not exercise the new shot-group badge/menu wiring or the atomic replace mutation, so the main risk surface remains untested.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` currently covers resize behavior only, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts` contains only helper tests.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the requested interaction against the existing timeline UI in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:343-364,774-803` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:107-201`. The repo’s established pattern for these affordances is a portal context menu, but the user request explicitly says clicking the badge should open a panel; the plan changes that to a menu/mini-menu, which is a product-level divergence that needs an explicit acceptance rather than being treated as equivalent.
flags.evidence: Checked the requested interaction against the existing timeline UI in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:343-364,774-803` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:107-201`. The repo’s established pattern for these affordances is a portal context menu, but the user request explicitly says clicking the badge should open a panel; the plan changes that to a menu/mini-menu, which is a product-level divergence that needs an explicit acceptance rather than being treated as equivalent.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked how shot groups are defined in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:29-33,73-75` and in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts:101-110`. The current repo only materializes shot groups when there are at least two matching clips, so the plan’s assumption that single-clip shots will not show the final-video badge is consistent with current code, but it still leaves one of the plan’s own open questions unresolved and narrows the feature unless that scope choice is confirmed.
flags.evidence: Checked how shot groups are defined in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:29-33,73-75` and in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts:101-110`. The current repo only materializes shot groups when there are at least two matching clips, so the plan’s assumption that single-clip shots will not show the final-video badge is consistent with current code, but it still leaves one of the plan’s own open questions unresolved and narrows the feature unless that scope choice is confirmed.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the proposed asset-registration call against the actual drag-drop contract in `src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts:48-55` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:66-85`. The plan says to call `registerGenerationAsset({ generationId: finalVideo.id, imageUrl: finalVideo.location, mimeType: 'video/mp4' })`, but `mimeType` is not a supported field on `GenerationDropData`, and `registerGenerationAsset()` only looks at `metadata.content_type`, `variantType`, or the URL suffix; as written, this call can misclassify the asset unless the location reliably ends with a video extension.
flags.evidence: Checked the proposed asset-registration call against the actual drag-drop contract in `src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts:48-55` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:66-85`. The plan says to call `registerGenerationAsset({ generationId: finalVideo.id, imageUrl: finalVideo.location, mimeType: 'video/mp4' })`, but `mimeType` is not a supported field on `GenerationDropData`, and `registerGenerationAsset()` only looks at `metadata.content_type`, `variantType`, or the URL suffix; as written, this call can misclassify the asset unless the location reliably ends with a video extension.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked `useShotGroups()` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:79-130` and the planned `TimelineCanvas` callback shape that only passes `shotId`. The repository can emit multiple distinct groups for the same shot across rows or separated runs on one row, so a switch action keyed only by `shotId` is ambiguous and can replace the wrong clip set; the callback needs the clicked group identity (`clipIds`, `rowId`, or a group key), not just the shot id.
flags.evidence: Checked `useShotGroups()` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:79-130` and the planned `TimelineCanvas` callback shape that only passes `shotId`. The repository can emit multiple distinct groups for the same shot across rows or separated runs on one row, so a switch action keyed only by `shotId` is ambiguous and can replace the wrong clip set; the callback needs the clicked group identity (`clipIds`, `rowId`, or a group key), not just the shot id.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked the existing video-drop paths in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/external-drop-utils.ts:46-55,386-412` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:198-252`. The broader repository pattern for video assets is not just “register then insert”; multi-generation video drops also patch `registry.duration` using `getDroppedGenerationDuration()`, while `useShotFinalVideos()` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37` currently fetches no duration metadata. That means final-video replacement is broader than badge wiring alone if the new clip is expected to behave like other video assets in timing UI and resize logic.
flags.evidence: Checked the existing video-drop paths in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/external-drop-utils.ts:46-55,386-412` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:198-252`. The broader repository pattern for video assets is not just “register then insert”; multi-generation video drops also patch `registry.duration` using `getDroppedGenerationDuration()`, while `useShotFinalVideos()` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37` currently fetches no duration metadata. That means final-video replacement is broader than badge wiring alone if the new clip is expected to behave like other video assets in timing UI and resize logic.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked shot-group action plumbing in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:343-364,757-803`. The canvas already has one shot-group menu state and portal renderer for group actions, so introducing a second parallel menu state for final-video actions would duplicate the same concern in the same component; extending the existing menu model with richer group payload looks more aligned with the current structure.
flags.evidence: Checked shot-group action plumbing in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:343-364,757-803`. flags.concern: Is there convincing verification for the change?: Checked for any existing tests around `useShotFinalVideos` or `shot_final_videos` in the editor paths and found none. A new `useFinalVideoAvailable` hook test would verify filtering and dismiss state, but it would still miss the highest-risk behavior: removing a specific group’s clips, inserting a single video clip, registering the asset with the right media type, and keeping the operation as one undoable edit.
